Title: Negative Immune Regulator CAD7 Functions as a Small-Molecule Aldehyde Reductase and Increases Histamine Accumulation in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAD (cinnamyl alcohol dehydrogenase) family; medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR) superfamily; NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Non-canonical CAD distinct from lignin-forming CADs; functions as a broad-spectrum small-molecule aldehyde reductase; overexpression elevates histamine and silencing elevates scopolin/chlorogenic acid via phenylpropanoid activation; also previously linked to GLV metabolism.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Histamine promotes plant susceptibility to Phytophthora parasitica
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression suppresses immunity and increases susceptibility to P. parasitica; silencing activates phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and silencing lines; in vitro enzyme assay; metabolomics (UPLC-MS/MS); targeted LC-MS/MS quantification; pathogen infection assays; inhibitor treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes histamine accumulation and increases susceptibility to Phytophthora parasitica; silencing has opposite metabolic/immune outcome
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora parasitica infection; exogenous histamine; histamine biosynthesis inhibitor BHOA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCAD7 is a non-canonical CAD that preferentially reduces diverse small aldehydes rather than lignin precursor p-coumaraldehyde. 2. AtCAD7 overexpression increases histamine accumulation, likely by reducing histamine-derived aldehydes and relieving inhibition on HDC. 3. Elevated histamine promotes Arabidopsis susceptibility to P. parasitica, while AtCAD7 silencing activates defense-associated phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for editing/silencing to reduce susceptibility and enhance resistance to Phytophthora pathogens by lowering histamine-associated susceptibility and boosting defense metabolites
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; Data available from corresponding author upon request

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: small-molecule aldehyde reductase / cinnamyl alcohol dehydrogenase 7
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAD family enzyme; medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase (MDR) superfamily; NADPH-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase-like enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Histamine metabolism / catabolism; also impacts phenylpropanoid-associated metabolic balance
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: side-route; catabolic/intermediate-processing step affecting feedback on histamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; acts as a key control point affecting histamine accumulation through aldehyde intermediate depletion
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: imidazole-4-carbaldehyde; 1-methylimidazole-4-carboxaldehyde; also (Z)-3-hexenal; acrolein; methylbutanal; isovaleraldehyde; benzaldehyde; 2-methoxybenzaldehyde; 3-methoxybenzaldehyde; salicylaldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: corresponding alcohols of the aldehyde substrates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses NADPH to reduce diverse small-molecule aldehydes, including histamine-derived aldehyde analogs, to the corresponding alcohols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts/interconverts histamine-derived aldehyde intermediates and thereby promotes target-product accumulation through feedback relief on histamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes susceptibility of Arabidopsis to Phytophthora parasitica
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. parasitica infection; exogenous histamine; BHOA histamine biosynthesis inhibitor
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally divergent from canonical lignin CAD AtCAD5; negligible activity toward p-coumaraldehyde; broad aldehyde specificity resembles bacterial YahK/YjgB more than lignin CADs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein expression and enzyme spectrophotometric assays; TLC product validation; overexpression/silencing in planta; metabolomics and LC-MS/MS metabolite quantification; disease assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases histamine accumulation and susceptibility to P. parasitica when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated histamine is associated with weakened immunity; silencing shifts metabolism toward defense-related phenylpropanoids/flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCAD7 catalyzes NADPH-dependent reduction of multiple small aldehydes, including histamine-derived aldehyde analogs. 2. Unlike canonical AtCAD5, it is not specialized for lignin precursor reduction. 3. By consuming histamine-derived aldehyde intermediates, AtCAD7 promotes histamine accumulation and disease susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing or editing AtCAD7 may be useful to improve resistance to Phytophthora by preventing histamine overaccumulation and favoring defense-metabolite buildup
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; Data available from corresponding author upon request

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes susceptibility to Phytophthora parasitica in the experimental system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in leaves for assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Treatment with imidazole-4-carbaldehyde and 1-methylimidazole-4-carboxaldehyde; P. parasitica infection after transient overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a mechanistic validation gene showing that increasing histamine biosynthesis raises susceptibility; aldehyde intermediates lowered NbHDC protein levels in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression of GFP-NbHDC in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS histamine quantification; infection assay; ELISA-based HDC protein detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases histamine accumulation and enhances susceptibility to P. parasitica
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher histamine correlates with greater disease susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbHDC is the rate-limiting enzyme for histamine biosynthesis. 2. Transient expression of NbHDC increases histamine content in N. benthamiana leaves. 3. Increased NbHDC expression enhances susceptibility to P. parasitica, supporting histamine as a susceptibility factor.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to suppress histamine-associated susceptibility, though not the main Arabidopsis focal gene in this study
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-like transient overexpression context via agroinfiltration in pART27-N system
